core_model.structure_summary: A first-order-in-time scalar evolution equation combining a nonlinear quadratic flux with a fractional-order dissipative operator, whose long-time behaviour is captured by scale-invariant self-similar source-type solutions.

variables.meaning: scalar field evolving in one spatial dimension
evolution parameter with a shifted origin
dissipation strength and fractional order of the nonlocal smoothing operator

notes: Two candidate cores: the hypoviscous scalar conservation law with fractional dissipation (primary, Burgers side) and a nonlocal integral operator (Hilbert/Riesz-type) governing the 2D companion equation; both share the self-similar-solution structure. Canonical form uses a fractional dissipative operator Lambda^alpha since the paper is 'hypoviscous' (order < 2). Equation extraction was noisy; the self-similar reduction and near-identity transformation are the paper's main analytical objects.
